title: Dictionary Basics Practice
instructions: Practice creating dictionaries, accessing values safely, adding/removing items, and checking key existence.
hints: Use copy() then update() to merge dictionaries
Use get() to safely access dictionary values
Loop through keys, checking if current value is dict
Use dictionary comprehension to swap: {v: k for k, v in d.items()}

testCases.description: merge_dicts should combine two dictionaries with dict2 overriding shared keys
testCases.validation: assert merge_dicts({'a': 1, 'b': 2}, {'b': 3, 'c': 4}) == {'a': 1, 'b': 3, 'c': 4}, 'merge_dicts should override shared keys with dict2 values'

testCases.description: safe_divide should divide values looked up by keys in the data dict
safe_divide should return 0 when a key is missing
testCases.validation: assert safe_divide({'x': 10}, 'x', 'y') == 0, 'safe_divide should return 0 when key is missing'

testCases.description: get_nested_value should traverse nested dictionaries using a list of keys
testCases.validation: assert get_nested_value({'a': {'b': {'c': 42}}}, ['a', 'b', 'c']) == 42, 'get_nested_value should return 42 for nested path [a, b, c]'

testCases.description: get_nested_value should return None when path does not exist
testCases.validation: assert get_nested_value({'a': 1}, ['a', 'b']) is None, 'get_nested_value should return None for invalid path'

testCases.description: invert_dict should swap keys and values
testCases.validation: assert invert_dict({'a': 1, 'b': 2}) == {1: 'a', 2: 'b'}, 'invert_dict should swap keys and values'

description: Practice fundamental dictionary operations in Python. Merge dictionaries using copy() and update(), safely access values with get() to handle missing keys, traverse nested dictionaries using a list of keys, and invert key-value pairs with dictionary comprehensions. These patterns form the foundation of working with key-value data structures.
